name: q19-nlp
documentation:
# Q19 NLP Library

A Python library for natural language processing tasks using Large Language Models (LLMs), focusing on structured information extraction and multilingual prompt generation.

## Features

- **Field Extraction**: Extract structured information from free-form text using LLMs
- **Multilingual Prompt Generation**: Generate natural, culturally-appropriate prompts in multiple languages
- **Flexible LLM Provider**: Support for different LLM backends (default: local LLaMA)
- **Robust JSON Processing**: Handles both well-formed JSON and fallback parsing
- **Conversation History Management**: Maintains and utilizes chat history for context

## Installation

With closed PyPI

```bash
pip install q19-nlp
```

## Documentation

For now, please access docs with

```bash
poetry run mkdocs serve
```

classes.name: q19_nlp.parsers.llm_parser.LLMParser

methods.name: q19_nlp.parsers.llm_parser.LLMParser.extract
documentation:
Extract structured information from a message using LLM.

Parameters
----------
history : list[dict[str, str]]
    Chat history containing previous interactions. Same as OpenAI API
field_names : dict[str, str]
    Dictionary mapping field names to their types. E.g. schema
message : str
    Input message to extract information from.

Returns
-------
tuple[dict[str, Any], list[dict[str, str]]]
    A tuple containing:
    - Dictionary of extracted fields and their values
    - Updated chat history after extraction

methods.name: q19_nlp.parsers.llm_parser.LLMParser.get_init_history
documentation: Get initial history for the LLM.

A parser that uses LLM to extract structured information from text.

Parameters
----------
llm_provider : LLMProvider
    Provider for LLM completion services.

Attributes
----------
llm : LLMProvider
    Instance of LLM provider for completions.
pm : PromptManager
    Manager for handling prompt templates.
postprocessor : BaseJsonPostprocessor
    Processor for parsing JSON responses.

functions.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.format_message
documentation:
Format a message with its role into a dictionary.

Parameters
----------
message : str
    The content of the message
role : str
    The role of the message sender (e.g., 'user', 'system', 'assistant')

Returns
-------
dict[str, str]
    A dictionary containing the role and content

functions.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.extractor
documentation:
Create an extraction prompt from user input and schema.

Parameters
----------
user_input : str
    The input text from the user
input_schema : dict[str, str]
    Schema defining the expected input fields
role : str
    The role of the message sender

Returns
-------
dict[str, str]
    Formatted message dictionary with the extraction prompt

functions.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.generator
documentation:
Create a generation prompt for a specific field.

Parameters
----------
field : str
    The field to generate content for
lang : str
    The target language
description : str or None
    Optional description of the field
role : str
    The role of the message sender

Returns
-------
dict[str, str]
    Formatted message dictionary with the generation prompt

classes.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager

methods.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager.__init__
documentation: Initialize the PromptManager with system prompts.

methods.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager.field_extraction_prompt
documentation:
Create a prompt for field extraction from user input.

Parameters
----------
input_schema : dict[str, str]
    Schema defining the expected input fields
user_input : str
    The input text from the user
history : list[dict[str, str]] or None
    Optional conversation history if none creates one

Returns
-------
dict[str, Any]
    Updated conversation history with the extraction prompt

methods.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager.field_asking_prompt
documentation:
Create a prompt for asking about a specific field.

Parameters
----------
field : str
    The field to ask about
lang : str
    The target language
description : str or None
    Optional description of the field
history : list[dict[str, str]] or None
    Optional conversation history if none creates one

Returns
-------
str
    Updated conversation history with the field-specific prompt

methods.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager.get_init_generation_history
documentation: Get initial history for the LLM.

methods.name: q19_nlp.prompts.base_pm.PromptManager.get_init_extraction_history
documentation: Get initial history for the LLM.

Manages prompt generation and field extraction for NLP tasks.

Attributes
----------
system_extract_prompt : Any
    System prompt for extraction loaded from YAML
system_generate_prompt : Any
    System prompt for generation loaded from YAML

methods.name: q19_nlp.postprocessor.base_json_postprocessor.BaseJsonPostprocessor.process
documentation:
Process a string response containing JSON-like data and extract specified fields.

Parameters
----------
response : str
    The input string containing JSON or JSON-like data, potentially wrapped in
    markdown code blocks (LLMs do that sometimes).
field_names : dict[str, str]
    A dictionary mapping field names to process.

Returns
-------
dict[str, Any]
    A dictionary containing the extracted field values. If JSON parsing fails,
    only the fields specified in field_names will be extracted.

Notes
-----
The method first attempts to parse the input as valid JSON. If that fails,
it falls back to line-by-line parsing looking for key-value pairs separated
by colons.

classes.name: q19_nlp.core.llm_provider.LLMConfig
documentation:
Interface for LLM config.

Parameters
----------
base_url : str
    The base URL for the LLM API.
api_key : str
    The API key for authentication.
model_name : str
    The name of the model to use.
temperature : float
    The sampling temperature to use.

classes.name: q19_nlp.core.llm_provider.BaseLLMConfig
documentation:
Configuration class for Language Model settings.

Parameters
----------
base_url : str, optional
    The base URL for the LLM API (default is "http://localhost:8000/v1").
api_key : str, optional
    The API key for authentication (default is "q19q19q19").
model_name : str, optional
    The name of the model to use (default is "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct").
temperature : float, optional
    The sampling temperature to use (default is 0.1).

classes.name: q19_nlp.core.llm_provider.LLMProvider

methods.name: q19_nlp.core.llm_provider.LLMProvider.get_completion
documentation:
Get a completion from the LLM based on the provided history.

Parameters
----------
history : list of dict
    A list of message dictionaries containing 'role' and 'content'. Same as in OpenAI API.

Returns
-------
tuple
    A tuple containing the completion result as a string and the updated history.

Provider class for interacting with the Language Model API.

Parameters
----------
config : LLMConfig, optional
    Configuration for the LLM (default is BaseLLMConfig).

classes.name: q19_nlp.generators.llm_generator.LLMGenerator

methods.name: q19_nlp.generators.llm_generator.LLMGenerator.generate
documentation:
Generate a response using the LLM based on given parameters.

Parameters
----------
field : str
    The field for which to generate a response.
lang : str
    The language in which to generate the response.
description : str or None
    Additional description or context for the generation, like type or smth like taht
history : list[dict[str, str]] or None
    Previous conversation history as a list of dictionaries. Same as OpenAI API format

Returns
-------
tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]
    A tuple containing:
    - The generated response as a string
    - The updated conversation history as a list of dictionaries

methods.name: q19_nlp.generators.llm_generator.LLMGenerator.get_init_history
documentation: Get initial history for the LLM.

A class to generate responses to users using a Language Learning Model (LLM) provider.

Parameters
----------
llm_provider : LLMProvider
    The LLM provider instance used for generating responses.

Attributes
----------
llm : LLMProvider
    The LLM provider instance.
pm : PromptManager
    The prompt manager instance for formatting prompts.
